refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic but slightly vague about the evaluation period and the nature of the availability target. Refining it to explicitly include the evaluation period and clarify that the availability target is customer-defined makes it clearer and maintains solution neutrality by not specifying how availability is achieved.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring and adaptive error correction
Design a robust link management protocol with automatic recovery and reconnection
Establish a prioritized resource allocation mechanism to maintain link quality under load
Utilize predictive maintenance and proactive fault detection for communication components
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and recovery mechanisms
coupling_check.notes: Selecting redundant communication paths as the DP promotes independence by isolating failure domains and enabling failover without requiring tight coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundant paths can be physically and logically separated to avoid common mode failures
Risk that redundancy increases system complexity and cost
Assumes operational policies allow for failover mechanisms and redundancy implementation
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and recovery mechanisms as secondary DPs to complement redundancy and ensure comprehensive link availability management.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall network and communication infrastructure responsible for managing communication paths and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles the physical and logical network connections used for communication paths and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health data about communication paths and failover status.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as redundant communication paths are core to network infrastructure; minimal diffusion as failover logic is centralized here.
Vehicle module uses communication but does not manage redundancy; would cause diffusion of failover logic across vehicle components.
Teleoperation depends on communication but is not responsible for managing redundant paths; containment weak and diffusion high.
Safety Services may monitor communication health but do not implement redundancy; partial containment but moderate diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant communication paths because it directly manages network infrastructure and failover mechanisms, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety monitoring requires access to communication redundancy status.
If network link interfaces are shared broadly, some diffusion of failover logic might occur outside NGHE Solution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks persist, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate failover mechanism implementation from monitoring functions, assigning monitoring to Safety Services and failover implementation strictly to NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant communication paths directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined teleoperation link availability target by providing failover capability, which is essential to meet the high availability acceptance criterion (≥ 0.999). This mapping is logically sound and grounded in the requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if safety monitoring requires access to communication redundancy status.
If network link interfaces are shared broadly, some diffusion of failover logic might occur outside NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2) for the DP, indicating strong cohesion and centralized failover logic. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary. However, some risk exists if monitoring functions or interface sharing cause diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the teleoperation link availability against the customer-defined target over the evaluation period, using telemetry data provided by the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear as the NGHE Solution module owns the implementation of redundant communication paths and failover mechanisms, with well-defined interfaces for network link and telemetry, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate failover mechanism implementation (assigned to NGHE Solution) from monitoring functions (assigned to Safety Services) to reduce diffusion risk.
Clarify interface boundaries and access rights to ensure failover logic remains centralized and not duplicated across modules.
Review and possibly restrict sharing of network link interfaces to prevent unintended diffusion of failover logic outside NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: Although all gates passed, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks between NGHE Solution and Safety Services modules necessitate refinement. Specifically, the DP should be refined to explicitly separate failover mechanism implementation from monitoring functions to reduce diffusion risk. Interface boundaries and access rights need clarification to ensure centralized failover logic and prevent duplication or unintended diffusion. These revisions will enhance architectural clarity and safety assurance before proceeding to next-level sub-FRs.

revise.why: To explicitly separate failover mechanism implementation (assigned to NGHE Solution) from monitoring functions (assigned to Safety Services) and clarify interface boundaries to reduce diffusion risk and improve containment.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary owner of failover logic implementation.
Safety Services module requires access to some monitoring functions that may overlap with failover status.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of failover logic due to shared network link interfaces and monitoring access.
Overlap between failover and safety monitoring functions causing architectural diffusion.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications between NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
Verification data showing centralized failover logic and absence of duplication.
Refined DP documentation separating failover and monitoring functions.
